name: DcomLaunch
description: Launches COM and DCOM servers in response to object activation requests. Foundational to the service host model.
security_note: DCOM is abused for lateral movement (e.g. MMC20.Application). Monitor remote DCOM activations.

name: RpcSs
description: The RPC subsystem that most Windows services rely on for inter-process communication.
security_note: A critical dependency for dozens of services; instability or tampering here cascades system-wide.

name: Winmgmt
description: Provides the WMI infrastructure for management data and operations across the system.
security_note: Heavily abused: WMI event subscriptions are fileless persistence, and WMI enables remote execution and lateral movement (T1047).

name: Schedule
description: Enables scheduled automatic execution of programs and scripts at defined times or triggers.
security_note: Scheduled tasks are a top persistence mechanism (T1053.005). Audit new/modified tasks in the Task Scheduler event log.

name: BITS
description: Transfers files in the background using idle network bandwidth (used by Windows Update).
security_note: BITS jobs can stealthily download payloads and provide persistence (T1197). Inspect with bitsadmin / Get-BitsTransfer.

name: Spooler
description: Manages print jobs and interacts with the printer. Runs as its own process, spoolsv.exe.
security_note: PrintNightmare (CVE-2021-34527) made this a marquee target. Disable it on servers that don't print.

name: WinDefend
description: The real-time antimalware protection service, backed by MsMpEng.exe.
security_note: Attackers try to stop or exclude it. Enable tamper protection and alert on service-stop / exclusion changes.

name: LanmanServer
description: Provides file, print and named-pipe sharing over SMB.
security_note: SMB drives lateral movement (PsExec, admin shares) and ransomware spread. Disable SMBv1 and restrict admin shares.

name: LanmanWorkstation
description: The SMB client: creates and maintains client network connections to remote servers.
security_note: Outbound SMB can leak NTLM hashes to attacker servers (forced authentication). Block outbound 445 at the perimeter.

name: TermService
description: Allows users to connect interactively to the machine over RDP.
security_note: RDP is a prime target for brute force and lateral movement. If enabled, enforce NLA, MFA and account lockout.

name: W32Time
description: Synchronises the system clock across the network. Accurate time is required for Kerberos.
security_note: Kerberos rejects tickets if clock skew exceeds 5 minutes, so time tampering can cause auth failures (relevant to Module 8).

name: Dnscache
description: Caches DNS names and registers the computer's name. Resolves and stores DNS lookups.
security_note: DNS is abused for C2 and exfiltration (DNS tunnelling, T1071.004). Log and baseline DNS query patterns (Module 10).

name: EventLog
description: Manages events and event logs, and supports logging by other components.
security_note: Attackers clear or stop logging to hide activity (T1070.001). Forward logs off-host and alert on Event ID 1102 (log cleared).

name: Themes
description: Provides user experience theme management (desktop visual styles).
security_note: Benign, but a good baseline example: cosmetic services should never run from non-System32 paths or unusual accounts.
